task_completion.description: Log in to the application using admin credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The login screen is bypassed, and the user is logged in as 'admin' (typically visible in the user profile section or top right corner of the UI).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the configuration settings for 'example-repo-local'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The configuration or settings page specifically for the repository named 'example-repo-local' is open in the application.

task_completion.description: Enter the exclusion pattern for .exe files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact string '**/*.exe' is visible inside the 'Excludes Pattern' (or equivalent file exclusion) input field.

task_completion.description: Enter the exclusion pattern for .dll files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact string '**/*.dll' is visible inside the 'Excludes Pattern' (or equivalent file exclusion) input field, typically alongside the .exe pattern.
A 'Save' or 'Apply' button is clicked, or a success notification indicating the repository configuration has been successfully updated is visible.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the application's graphical user interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show navigation through the web interface (e.g., Artifactory/JFrog UI) rather than just executing API curl commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent modified the actual repository settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exclusion patterns are typed into the correct configuration fields within the application, not in a separate text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser developer console or 'Inspect Element' panel is not open to manipulate the DOM.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target repository is exactly 'example-repo-local'. The required exclusion patterns are '**/*.exe' and '**/*.dll'. These must be added to the repository's 'Excludes Pattern' configuration. The system backend will verify that .txt files are accepted and .exe/.dll files are rejected.
